channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Pathogenesis & Basic Science
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Pathogenesis & Basic Science

item.title: Inflammatory bowel diseases 2026: form, function and therapeutic considerations for the epithelial barrier.
WNT2B impairs endosomal trafficking via WASHC5 to inhibit autophagy: a novel non-secretory WNT pathway.
item.description: Autophagy - WNT2B is canonically characterized as a secreted WNT-family ligand, which is transported to the extracellular space via the endoplasmic reticulum (ER)-Golgi pathway and binds to cell surface FZDs (...

item.title: Tryptophan 2,3-dioxygenase deficiency attenuates dextran sodium sulphate -induced ulcerative colitis in mice by suppressing macrophage polarisation and reducing intestinal epithelial cell apoptosis.
item.description: British journal of pharmacology - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic, nonspecific inflammatory bowel disease. The kynurenine pathway (KP) of tryptophan metabolism has been implicated in several autoimmune diseases, including mult...

item.title: Human colon organoid Transwell model for investigating epithelial growth and repair.
item.description: npj biomedical innovations - Disruption of the intestinal epithelial barrier is a hallmark of Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD), yet mechanisms of epithelial repair remain poorly understood. We developed a scalable human colon ...

item.title: Lactose-decorated, alginate-coated chitosan nanoparticles loaded with Coomassie Brilliant Blue G-250 protect colonic barrier integrity in experimental colitis.
item.description: Tissue & cell - Ulcerative colitis involves epithelial injury, crypt distortion, barrier failure, and inflammatory activation within colonic tissue. To support local delivery of the P2X7R antagonist Coomassie Bril...

item.title: Transcriptional Modulation of the Nrf2 Antioxidant Pathway and Tight Junctions by a Plant Sterol Food Supplement in an Intestinal Inflammation Coculture Model.
item.description: Journal of food science - There is growing evidence supporting the anti-inflammatory activity of plant sterols (PS) in preclinical models of intestinal inflammation. However, the underlying mechanisms driving these benefici...

item.title: Acrylamide Exposure Activates EGFR-PI3K in Intestinal Macrophages to Promote the Onset and Progression of Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Journal of biochemical and molecular toxicology - To investigate the impact and mechanisms of acrylamide (AA), an environmental pollutant, on inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). We exposed normal mice and chronic colitis mouse models to AA and exami...

item.title: Liandan Xiaoyan Formula ameliorates DSS-induced ulcerative colitis through altering the metabolism of bile acids by regulating the FXR-FGF15 signaling pathway.
item.description: Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic, non-specific inflammatory disorder that primarily affects the rectal and colonic mucosa and submucosa, and its pathogenesis is closely related to the dysregula...

item.title: Codonopsis pilosula as the sovereign herb in Shenling Baizhu formula ameliorates DSS-induced ulcerative colitis via spermidine-AMPK-mediated mitophagy to inhibit the cGAS-STING inflammatory axis.
item.description: International immunopharmacology - Dextran sulfate sodium-induced ulcerative colitis is featured by excessive intestinal inflammation and disrupted mucosal immune homeostasis. Codonopsis pilosula is the sovereign herb in Shenling Ba...

item.title: RNA Helicase DDX21 Controls CD4+ T Cell Proliferation and Promotes Inflammatory Bowel Disease via Translational Control.
item.description: Advanced science (Weinheim, Baden-Wurttemberg, Germany) - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is characterized by dysregulated T cell responses. RNA helicases, including DExD-box helicase 21 (DDX21), are pivotal in RNA metabolism, but their role in T cell-me...

item.title: Β-Resorcylaldehyde attenuates DSS-induced colitis by inhibiting inflammation and oxidative stress through the Keap1/Nrf2/HO-1 pathway.
item.description: International immunopharmacology - Ulcerative colitis (UC), a bowel condition marked by heightened oxidative stress, ongoing inflammation, and intestinal barrier dysfunction, has limited therapeutic options that are both effective a...

item.title: Loganin alleviates oxidative stress-induced apoptosis by modulating mitochondrial function and STAT3 signaling in DSS-induced colitis and H2O2-injured Caco-2 cells.
item.description: Scientific reports - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease characterized by recurrent inflammation and mucosal damage. Loganin, a natural iridoid glycoside with anti-inflammatory and antioxida...

item.title: MCT1-Mediated Lactate Transport Regulates Ferroptosis in Ulcerative Colitis via Histone Lactylation.
item.description: Free radical biology & medicine - Ferroptosis, an iron-dependent type of controlled cell death caused by lipid peroxidation, contributes to intestinal epithelial injury in ulcerative colitis (UC). Monocarboxylate transporter 1 (MCT...

item.title: Macrophage USP9X attenuates colitis by restricting oncostatin M production via K27-linked deubiquitination of STAT1.
item.description: Cellular & molecular immunology - Dysregulated posttranslational modifications drive inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) pathogenesis, yet the specific roles of deubiquitinases in intestinal macrophages remain poorly characterized. He...

item.title: Dietary acrylamide exposure increases susceptibility to ulcerative colitis: A comprehensive analysis from network toxicology to in vivo experimental validation.
item.description: Food and chemical toxicology : an international journal published for the British Industrial Biological Research Association - Acrylamide (AA) is a contaminant present in foods cooked at high temperatures, and its formation is associated with the Maillard reaction. Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory disease ...

item.title: Tangeretin, a bioactive polymethoxyflavone from immature Citrus aurantium L. fruit, ameliorates experimental colitis by targeting the macrophage β2 integrin Mac-1.
item.description: Journal of ethnopharmacology - Zhiqiao, the dried immature fruit of Citrus aurantium L., is a traditional Chinese medicine widely used for treating gastrointestinal disorders. Among its varieties, Quzhiqiao, a geo-authentic herb...

item.title: MODULATING OXIDATIVE STRESS AND INFLAMMATION: SODIUM BUTYRATE'S THERAPEUTIC PROMISE IN EXPERIMENTAL COLITIS.
item.description: Arquivos de gastroenterologia - Oxidative stress and excessive cytokine signalling are central to the pathogenesis of ulcerative colitis (UC). Sodium butyrate (SB) has emerged as a potential therapeutic candidate due to its antio...

item.title: B3GNT7 regulates mucin glycosylation and protects against colitis and infection.
item.description: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology - Mucus covers and protects colonic epithelial cells. Mucus is mainly composed of heavily O-glycosylated proteins called mucins, and disruption of normal mucin glycosylation occurs in ulcerative coli...
